name: USS Abraham Lincoln (CVN-72)
detail: Arabian Sea. F/A-18s, F-35Cs. Now sole active US carrier in theater after USS Ford fire.

name: USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN-78) — FIRE / DEPARTING
detail: Sustained 30-hour onboard fire Mar 18, departing theater for emergency repairs at NATO facility. Leaves USS Lincoln as sole US carrier in theater. Iran claims missile strike; US Navy cites 'internal mechanical incident.' Cause under investigation. No confirmed sailor fatalities from fire.

name: F-15E Strike Eagle
detail: 3 lost to friendly fire in Kuwait

name: KC-135 Stratotanker — Crashed Mar 13
detail: 4th US aircraft lost in conflict. Crashed in western Iraq supporting Epic Fury ops. CENTCOM: 'not due to hostile or friendly fire.' Islamic Resistance in Iraq claims shootdown. 6 crew onboard, no ejection seats.

name: French Aircraft Carrier (CDG)
detail: French carrier joined US CENTCOM area. Pro-Iranian Iraq groups threatened French interests. French soldier CWO Arnaud Frion killed in Mala Qara base drone strike Mar 13.

name: USS George H.W. Bush (CVN-77) — ARRIVED CENTCOM AOR Apr 24
detail: USS George H.W. Bush (CVN-77) entered the US Central Command area of responsibility on April 23–24, 2026, bringing total US carriers in the Middle East to THREE — the highest since 2003. Left Norfolk March 31, transited around the Cape of Good Hope (avoiding Houthis in Red Sea). Accompanied by three Arleigh Burke destroyers, an amphibious assault ship with ~2,500 Marines, and a dock landing ship. Carries CVW-8 with F/A-18 Super Hornets and F-35Cs. Now joined by USS Abraham Lincoln (CVN-72) in Arabian Sea for full theater coverage.

name: USS Chief (MCM-14) & USS Pioneer (MCM-9) — DEPLOYED TO GULF
detail: Avenger-class mine countermeasure vessels USS Chief and USS Pioneer were spotted transiting toward the Gulf region in mid-April as contingency for Iranian mine-laying operations in the Strait of Hormuz. Trump on April 23 ordered mine-clearing operations tripled. Pentagon assessment: clearing all Iranian mines from the strait could take approximately six months even after cessation of hostilities. Autonomous minehunting systems and mine-hunting helicopters also deployed.

name: Ukraine — 201 Military Experts Deployed to Gulf
detail: Ukraine deployed 201 military experts to Gulf states on Day 19 to assist with countering Iranian drones, drawing on Ukrainian battlefield experience with Russian-supplied Shahed-type drones. Significant development in broader coalition-building around US Gulf operations.

name: 82nd Airborne Division (~1,000+) — DEPLOYED
detail: Elements of the 82nd Airborne Division (1st BCT + HQ staff + enablers, ~1,000+ paratroopers) deployed to CENTCOM AOR. Maj. Gen. Brandon Tegtmeier and division staff included. Mission unclear — analysts note possible Kharg Island seizure scenario (handles ~90% of Iranian oil exports). Total US forces in region now ~55,000+.

name: USS Tripoli (LHA-7) — 3,500 MARINES ARRIVED
detail: USS Tripoli (LHA-7) America-class amphibious assault ship arrived in theater on March 29 with approximately 3,500 US Marines aboard. The deployment substantially increases US amphibious and ground assault capability. Analysts note the force package is consistent with a Kharg Island seizure mission or Hormuz enforcement operation — both scenarios Trump publicly referenced on March 29. Washington Post reported Pentagon preparing for 'weeks of ground operations,' potentially including special forces raids and conventional infantry. Iranian Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf warned a ground invasion would be 'met with force.'
